career_angle: Modal's serverless GPU/compute model is worth learning if you're building ML pipelines -- but evaluate it against Ray, Celery, or plain Kubernetes jobs before buying the marketing.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: First-party blog post -- they're announcing their own product, so inherently promotional

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: No benchmarks, no comparison to alternatives, no customer case studies with numbers in the claim itself

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Modal is a real product you can sign up for and test, but '1 million jobs' claim lacks defined job size or complexity

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Company announcing its own paid product -- maximum commercial incentive to oversimplify and overpromise

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Batch processing at scale is solved problem space; Modal's approach is one of many valid options, not breakthrough

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: '1 line of code' and '1 million jobs' are marketing anchors, not technical specifications -- no mention of latency, cost, or failure rates

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Modal has shipped real infrastructure used by ML teams; they're not vaporware, but they do lean into hype-driven launches
